figures.caption: Figure 1: CUA-SUITE Overview. Human GUI trajectories are recorded across desktop platforms, expert-verified, and annotated with keyframes, bounding boxes, and interaction logs. The resulting CUA-SUITE comprises UI-VISION, a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate grounding and planning capabilities of computer-use agents; GROUNDCUA, which consists of densely labeled UI screenshots with 3.6 million annotations; VIDEOCUA, which comprises 55 hours of video of humans performing computer-use tasks with detailed action trajectories.

Table 2: Comparison of VIDEOCUA with existing GUI trajectory and agent datasets. VIDEOCUA is the only large-scale, human-curated dataset providing continuous 30 fps expert video for professional desktop applications with long multi-layered CoT annotations. Video: continuous video recordings released with the dataset (not per-step screenshots). CoT: chain-of-thought annotations (ℓ= long multi-layered, s= short). Though not released directly, VIDEOCUA has annotations dense enough for CoT generation with the code released. Scale: approximate data volume (SS = screenshots; h = video hours; fr. = video frames). OmniACT provides single-step tasks. OSWorld is an evaluation benchmark without training trajectories. “–” indicates not reported.
Table 5: UI element categories in CUA-SUITE with descriptions and representative examples.

Table 6: Per-Application Performance (OpenCUA-32B). Applications exhibiting the lowest and highest @50px success rates (minimum 3 coordinate predictions). Performance varies widely, from under 5% to over 70%, reflecting the diversity of desktop application interfaces.
Table 4: Categories of desktop applications and their corresponding applications.
Figure 2: Representative Prediction Failures. Green circles indicate ground truth and red circles show model predictions. These examples illustrate desktop-specific challenges for CUAs.
Table 7: Distribution of Prediction Distances >50px. Both models show a substantial proportion of high-distance predictions, with roughly one-third exceeding 500 pixels.

Table 1: Element Grounding Performance on UI-VISION. Models are ranked in descending order by their average score. Results are drawn from their respective original publications, with the exception of Qwen3-VL, which is reported as evaluated in Zhou et al. (2025b).
